_cite.reason: URL-normalization 1c: strip trailing markdown-emphasis `*`/backtick glued to citation URLs in intelligence_item_sections.content_md (customer-checkable link + validate_item_provenance criterion-2 exact-compare). Operator ruling 2026-07-04. Same transform as the write-site fix.

prior.content_md:
There are no new EPR or producer-registration obligations created by the Riigikogu May 11, 2026 session items as they relate to the workspace. The legislative measures under discussion are fiscal (excise duty and VAT rate amendments) rather than product-registration or extended-producer-responsibility schemes.

**Existing registration context relevant to the workspace:**

The Estonian Tax and Customs Board (EMTA) administers excise duty registration for fuel-sector operators. An excise warehouse keeper must hold a permit issued by EMTA to produce, process, store, receive, and dispatch excise goods under a suspension arrangement. A registered consignee must obtain EMTA authorisation to receive fuel transported from another EU Member State under excise suspension for commercial purposes. A certified consignor must register with EMTA for each consignment dispatched to another Member State via the EMCS or SADHES information systems. These roles apply to fuel traders and logistics operators handling bonded fuel — the workspace should confirm whether its operations (e.g., managing generator fuel for film or live-events productions crossing Estonian borders) trigger any of these registration requirements.

**VAT registration threshold:** Estonian VAT registration is required for businesses with turnover exceeding €40,000. If the workspace conducts taxable supplies in Estonia above this threshold, VAT registration is mandatory and fuel purchases are subject to the standard 24% rate with input VAT recovery available for business use.

**Promised but not yet released:** If the Centre Party's motor fuel VAT bill (first reading May 11, 2026) advances, EMTA would be expected to issue implementing guidance on how a reduced 13% rate applies to mixed fuel purchases (e.g., vehicles used partly for business, partly for other purposes) and on transitional invoicing requirements. No such guidance has been published as of the date of this item.

*Source: Estonian Tax and Customs Board — Fuel and Electricity excise guidance, EMTA. https://www.emta.ee/en/business-client/taxes-and-payment/excise-duties/fuel-and-electricity. PwC Worldwide Tax Summaries — Estonia Corporate Other Taxes (current). https://taxsummaries.pwc.com/estonia/corporate/other-taxes.*
